danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy shamans and unit formations visible on 3D terrain; distinct color-coded groups (green Matak, yellow Chumara, red Dakini)
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat requiring immediate attention or strategic response

danger_signals.visual_form: Military buildings and towers built by opposing tribes in proximity to player territory
Visual effects of destructive spells (tornados, storms, volcanos) originating from enemy positions
danger_signals.meaning: Direct magical threat; environmental hazard created by hostile action

danger_signals.visual_form: Altered landscape, raised/lowered terrain, environmental changes in combat zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue-colored units and structures clustered in consolidated territories; visible shaman presence
Neutral characters clustering around trees and water sources without hostile engagement
safety_signals.meaning: Peaceful resource presence; convertible population available for expansion

safety_signals.visual_form: Fully constructed huts, towers, and military structures with visible follower activity
Player shaman positioned at spell totem locations
safety_signals.meaning: Magic regeneration and spell access available; power accumulation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player shaman character model; distinctive blue unit silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Blue-tinted structures and territory; blue aura around controlled units

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Green color distinguishes all Matak units from other enemy tribes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Green-colored structures; green terrain/environmental tinting in Matak-controlled zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Yellow color distinguishes all Chumara units and structures
Red color distinguishes all Dakini units and structures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct neutral character models without faction color coding
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct from faction-aligned units; no combat capability indicated
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cluster formations around trees and water features; distinct visual separation from faction territories

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large swathes of neutral terrain with wildmen populations; minimal faction infrastructure; variable color-coded territories emerging
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player shaman begins on planets furthest from sun; limited enemy presence; wildmen available for conversion via Convert spell

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple color-coded factions visible simultaneously on same world; overlapping territory claims; battlefield scarring from spell effects (terrain deformation, raised/lowered landmasses)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy shamans actively visible; military units engaged in formation; spell effects (tornados, storms, volcanos) dynamically reshaping terrain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One faction's color (blue for player) visually dominates territory; enemy structure density reduced; consolidated settlement patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defeated enemy shamans eliminated from world; surviving enemy units fragmentary; player units freely move through claimed zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player shaman presence visually emphasized; spell effects more prominent; terrain manipulation increasing in scale and frequency
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shaman can reincarnate if killed; spell repertoire expanding throughout campaign; twenty-six spells gradually learned; mana-based magic system visually depleting/regenerating

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual transition between distinct planetary environments; camera rotation enables full 360-degree view of spherical world topology; torus terrain locally projected as planet
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign advances through twenty-five unnamed planets sequentially; new enemy alliances emerge on different worlds; new spell acquisition gates narrative progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Green Matak, yellow Chumara, red Dakini, and blue player tribe visually distinguished by consistent color identity across all units, structures, and territories — immediately communicates alliance/hostility without UI explanation

Spell effects (Tornado, Volcano, Landbridge) leave permanent visual scars on landscape — raised/lowered terrain, destroyed buildings, and reshaped coastlines document previous battles and territorial struggles
Neutral population clusters around trees and water sources indicate resource distribution and convertible population pools; visual density of wildmen reveals recruitment potential
Visible shaman placement relative to structures and totems communicates faction control centers and magical power sources; enemy shaman visibility indicates primary threat vectors
Concentration of huts, towers, and military buildings visually indicates faction economic strength and military readiness; sparse structure placement suggests weakened or newly-arrived faction
3D spherical world visualization (despite torus topology) creates sense of isolated planetary arenas; visual separation between twenty-five planets marks narrative progression steps

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visibly constructing huts, towers, and military buildings; performing resource gathering from trees; trainable into specialized combat units
Visually engaged in close-range combat formations; attacking enemy units and structures; clustering near combat zones
Positioned at distance from melee combat; visible projectile effects toward enemies; weaker individual unit visually compensated by range advantage
Approaching enemy units or wildmen; conversion spell effects visible as unit color change from enemy/neutral to player faction color; preventing enemy preachers from converting friendly troops
Moving subtly through enemy territory; gathering intelligence from enemy positions; performing covert functions with minimal visible engagement

Visibly distinct from regular units; approaching totems to regenerate/learn spells; casting spells (Tornado, Storm, Volcano, Swarm, Landbridge) with dramatic visual effects; reincarnating if killed; gradually becoming more visually powerful
Visible on battlefield casting destructive spells; leading tribe against player; eliminated when faction is defeated; each displayed in distinct faction color
Clustering peacefully around trees/water without combat engagement; visually vulnerable to conversion via player Convert spell; no aggressive action capability

notes: Populous: The Beginning uses consistent color-coding across factions as primary visual communication system. The transition from isometric (previous games) to 3D with 360-degree rotatable camera enables more nuanced environmental storytelling through terrain manipulation and spatial relationships. Spell effects serve dual function: mechanical gameplay impact and visual narrative of magical power progression. The spherical planetary visualization (despite underlying torus topology) reinforces isolated arena combat structure. Faction hostility communicated primarily through color identity and active combat engagement rather than explicit UI markers. Shaman visibility and positioning becomes key danger/safety signal — player shaman reincarnation mechanic visually reinforces godhood progression narrative.
